title: PTD Today — Media

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Hinge announced a $1 million fund to support social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London to host free or affordable in-person social events for young people. The program is a global expansion of Hinge’s One More Hour social impact initiative and is framed as a response to rising loneliness among Gen Z.
bullets: Gen Z facing increasing loneliness, per the description.
Hinge is committing $1 million to social groups to subsidize events.
Target cities: New York, Los Angeles, and London.
Events are intended to be free or affordable for young people.
This expands Hinge’s One More Hour social impact program globally.
takeaways: Major dating platforms are funding in-person, city-level events—monitor localized demand patterns tied to such initiatives.
Event coordination backed by apps may increase short-term spikes in registration or app activity in specific metro areas.
Prioritize capacity planning and regional monitoring for NY, LA, and London to accommodate potential shifts in user engagement tied to offline events.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This AWS Events session demonstrates how to automatically build a knowledge graph from raw documents and connect it to an AI agent so the agent understands your company. The talk is an end-to-end demo that requires no graph expertise.
bullets: Knowledge graphs address AI agents' lack of company-specific knowledge.
Build a knowledge graph automatically from raw documents.
Connect the knowledge graph to an agent that understands YOUR business.
No need to learn graph queries or graph theory.
End-to-end demo; references first episode comparing VectorDB vs GraphDB.
takeaways: So what: You can provide AI agents with accurate company/domain knowledge by ingesting internal documents into a knowledge graph.
So what: Eliminates the need to train staff in graph queries/theory, lowering the technical barrier to deployment.
So what: An automated, end-to-end build can speed up rolling out enterprise agents that leverage your organization’s data.
So what: The session situates knowledge graphs alongside VectorDB/GraphDB considerations (see linked first episode) for database choice context.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Randa Barsoum, Tax Partner at PwC US, notes that energy incentives are often top of mind but represent only part of the tax opportunity for sustainability projects. She highlights other avenues—state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation—that can help unlock meaningful tax value from sustainability investments.
bullets: Energy incentives are important but not the only tax opportunity for sustainability.
State and local abatements can support sustainability initiatives.
Location-based incentives may influence project siting and economics.
R&D tax credits can apply to innovation like reimagined packaging or processes.
Viewing sustainability through a broader tax lens can create additional tax value.
takeaways: Evaluate state and local abatements when planning grid or data center locations to improve project economics.
Consider location-based incentives as part of site selection and expansion decisions.
Explore R&D tax credits for operational innovations (e.g., cooling, packaging, processes) that support sustainability goals.
Integrate tax-credit opportunities into sustainability investment planning to maximize after-tax returns.

items.title: Bringing In $251K A Year With 3 Jobs In The Bay Area

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Naseema McElroy is a 44-year-old labor and delivery nurse in Brentwood, California who runs a personal finance brand called Financially Intentional and brings in $251K a year across three jobs. She accumulated nearly $1 million in debt in 2015, documented her debt-payoff journey through her business, and was debt-free by 2017.
bullets: Naseema McElroy — 44, labor and delivery nurse and founder of Financially Intentional.
Based in Brentwood, California; title notes earnings from three jobs in the Bay Area totaling $251K/year.
Accumulated nearly $1M in debt in 2015 and documented her payoff journey publicly.
Started Financially Intentional to chronicle debt payoff; became debt-free by 2017.
Segment is part of CNBC Make It's Millennial Money series; full production credits listed.
takeaways: Revenue-generating personal brands that document journeys produce ongoing digital content — plan for scalable hosting, streaming and CDN capacity to support video delivery.
Professionals combining traditional employment with content businesses increase demand for reliable cloud storage and backup for media assets.
Media segments with professional production needs benefit from robust ingest, editing and secure collaboration pipelines — prioritize low-latency transfers and high-availability services.
Growth of individual creators in high-cost regions underscores need for flexible, scalable infrastructure that can handle spikes in audience and content uploads.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, NVIDIA's GeForce Garage video announces the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build Contest (u/JeffersonPutnam), who built a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 system. The description lists the full winning system specs and links to the PCPartPicker build and the GeForce host (Dwight / @_MightyPotato).
bullets: Winner: Reddit user u/JeffersonPutnam — built an SFF GeForce RTX 5090 rig.
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2; GPU: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition.
Platform: MSI MPG B850I Edge (mini‑ITX) with Ryzen 7 9800X3D and 32GB DDR5‑6000.
Power and cooling: Corsair SF1000 SFX PSU and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
PCPartPicker link provided for full parts list; host credited as @_MightyPotato.
takeaways: Compact SFF integration: a Fractal Era 2 chassis was used to house a flagship RTX 5090, showing high-end GPUs can be paired with small‑form‑factor cases when matched with appropriate components listed.
Power delivery: the build uses a Corsair SF1000 SFX PSU, highlighting the need for high‑capacity SFX power in compact, high‑power systems.
Cooling considerations: a Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO was selected for thermal management in the SFF layout.
Platform choice: a mini‑ITX motherboard (MSI MPG B850I Edge) and a Ryzen 7 9800X3D were chosen, demonstrating mainstream high‑performance CPU and memory (DDR5‑6000) selections for compact builds.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description, TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy GenAI solutions using Google Cloud’s infrastructure. The collaboration streamlined operations, boosted developer productivity, compressed the company’s digital transformation timeline, and unlocked new revenue streams.
bullets: TTEC worked with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure were central to the implementation.
Efforts focused on streamlining operations and increasing developer productivity.
The partnership compressed TTEC’s digital transformation timeline.
New revenue streams and expanded business ambitions were unlocked.
takeaways: Robust cloud infrastructure can speed AI prototyping and shorten time-to-value.
AI deployment that boosts developer productivity can reduce operational bottlenecks.
Cloud consulting partnerships can compress transformation timelines for faster ROI.
Deploying GenAI can create opportunities for new revenue streams and business expansion.
